proposed_from.topic: intrinsic minimax modulus (functional modulus of continuity) for finite-dimensional functionals of no-solution NPIV least-squares projections, indexed by spectral-decay, source, and functional-representer source classes — the missing converse to the Tikhonov achievability rate of Shen-Kallus et al (arXiv:2604.24660); deliver a sharp intrinsic-modulus characterization with matched lower and upper bounds where available, and claim Tikhonov rate-suboptimality only if an estimator attaining the faster modulus rate is exhibited
proposed_from.novelty_justification: The closest published upper-bound result is ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV, which proves Tikhonov achievability for the no-solution projection target but not a converse. The closest published minimax papers for linear NPIV functionals, BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR, assume the classical exact-solution target. The exact gap is therefore not 'another NPIV modulus paper' but the missing intrinsic modulus for a different estimand: finite-dimensional linear functionals of the minimum-norm least-squares projection when the inverse equation may have no solution. The retained core is sharp because it separates three objects the literature currently conflates: the realized-functional frontier R_n^*(psi), the class envelope bar_R_n(b), and the root-n local-Riesz condition sum_j psi_j^2/sigma_j^2 < infinity. That is a real converse result, not a relabelling of Tikhonov upper rates.
seed_list: Functional-specific intrinsic modulus R_n^*(psi) for no-solution NPIV projection functionals, with a root-n iff frontier in the local Riesz norm.
Worst-case representer-envelope bar_R_n(b) over representer source ellipsoids, separating realized-functional difficulty from class difficulty.
Polynomial and exponential no-solution phase diagrams for bar_R_n(b), mirroring but not assuming the exact-solution NPIR frontier.
First-order Tikhonov saturation theorem: identify source/representer regimes where Tikhonov is slower than the intrinsic modulus and exhibit a higher-qualification estimator.
Honest confidence-interval length theorem: the no-solution intrinsic modulus is also the CI benchmark for linear projection functionals.
Ordered-modulus adaptive estimator and adaptive honest inference over nested spectral/source/representer classes.
Between-class adaptation impossibility in irregular mildly ill-posed no-solution regimes, with necessary sqrt(log n) loss.
Exact-solution reduction theorem: when g belongs to range(T), the no-solution modulus collapses to the Breunig-Johannes exact-solution formulas.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:gaussian-sequence / all rate statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The review accepts the singular-coordinate Gaussian model as a reduced experiment, not as a full i.i.d. NPIV theorem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All minimax and adaptive claims are conditional Gaussian-sequence claims until an asymptotic-equivalence or embedding theorem is added.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:projection-domain / h^Pi
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The review accepts g^R in range(T) as the Shen-style least-squares nonemptiness/domain condition, but it makes h^Pi an exact solution for the projected reduced form.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline is bound to the projected exact equation T h^Pi = g^R and does not characterize extra difficulty created by the orthogonal no-solution residual r.

seed_details.open_problem: Intrinsic minimax modulus for finite-dimensional linear functionals of the minimum-norm no-solution NPIV projection.
seed_details.kernel: Define the functional-specific oracle frontier R_n^*(psi), prove c R_n^*(psi) <= omega_n(psi) <= C R_n^*(psi), and derive root-n regularity iff sum_j psi_j^2/sigma_j^2 < infinity.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from Chen-Reiss once the problem is written in singular coordinates.
seed_details.rebuttal: Chen-Reiss treats whole-function loss and exact-solution structure; the missing step is the no-solution projection geometry plus a functional-specific converse indexed jointly by projected-source and representer structure.

seed_details.open_problem: Class-level envelope for no-solution linear functionals under representer ellipsoids.
seed_details.kernel: After the realized-functional modulus is known, characterize bar_R_n(b) and show existence of least-favorable representers achieving that envelope up to constants.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the Breunig-Johannes exact-solution envelope with h_0 renamed h^Pi.
seed_details.rebuttal: The class lives on projected coordinates theta_j^Pi of a least-squares projection target that remains defined without primal solvability; exact-solution NPIR does not cover that target.

seed_details.open_problem: Explicit mild/severe ill-posed rate formulas for no-solution functionals.
seed_details.kernel: Specialize bar_R_n(b) under polynomial and exponential sigma_j decay and polynomial source/representer profiles to obtain explicit phase diagrams.
seed_details.referee_objection: A routine corollary once the modulus is proved.
seed_details.rebuttal: True as a standalone flagship claim; it survives only as a corollary package attached to s1, not as the main angle.

seed_details.open_problem: Tikhonov may be qualification-limited relative to the intrinsic modulus.
seed_details.kernel: Compare first-order Tikhonov to iterated Tikhonov or spectral cutoff for no-solution projection functionals and prove a strict rate gap where qualification binds.
seed_details.referee_objection: Without an explicit feasible estimator and matched upper bound, this is just inverse-problem folklore about saturation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection holds at present; no explicit no-solution estimator proof is ready.

seed_details.open_problem: Modulus-governed honest CI length for no-solution projection functionals.
seed_details.kernel: Transfer the intrinsic modulus to an optimal CI-length benchmark, then align it with the no-solution Gaussian singular experiment.
seed_details.referee_objection: This adds inference packaging but no new functional characterization beyond the modulus theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; it is a consumer theorem after s1, not the kernel.

seed_details.open_problem: Adaptive estimation over unknown spectral/source/representer classes.
seed_details.kernel: Construct an ordered-Lepski selector calibrated to the no-solution ordered modulus and prove regime-dependent exact or sqrt(log n)-loss adaptation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is premature unless the base modulus and class envelope are already settled.
seed_details.rebuttal: Valid objection for a standalone angle; it survives only as the open conjecture layer after s1.

seed_details.open_problem: Necessary adaptation loss in irregular mildly ill-posed no-solution regimes.
seed_details.kernel: Show the ordered no-solution between-class modulus forces sqrt(log n) loss exactly in the irregular mildly ill-posed region.
seed_details.referee_objection: Derivative of classical NPIV adaptation results once the class envelope is known.
seed_details.rebuttal: Yes; it is a fine corollary or second paper, not the first kernel.

seed_details.open_problem: Recover exact-solution NPIR formulas as a no-solution special case.
seed_details.kernel: Under g in range(T), show h^Pi=h_0 and the no-solution modulus collapses to Breunig-Johannes formulas.
seed_details.rebuttal: Agreed; needed as a corollary for well-posedness, not as the angle.

proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","message":"Patched the flagged items in the proposal core at the requested path: added the explicit no-solution least-squares domain condition via the range component g^R in range(T), redefined h^Pi through that law-level decomposition, rewrote the adaptive conjecture as a per-regime bound with no free t-index, and demoted the root-n statement to a Bennett-equivalence recovery with propagated comparator-table and related-work updates. Verified that the revised artifact parses as JSON and that ass:projection-domain is propagated through every affected statement.","artifacts":["/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/stat_npiv_nosol_modulus/discovery/stat_npiv_nosol_modulus_proto_core.json"],"literature_checklist":[{"author":"Zikai Shen, Nathan Kallus, Dimitri Meunier, Houssam Zenati, Arthur Gretton, and Aurelien Bibaut","year":2026,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV","one_line":"Defines the no-solution NPIV least-squares projection target and motivates the explicit least-squares domain condition now built into the revised converse theorem.","relevant_to":"thm:functional-modulus"},{"author":"Christoph Breunig and Jan Johannes","year":2009,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR","one_line":"Exact-solution NPIR linear-functional minimax benchmark used for the continuity domain, representer envelope comparison, and solvable-case reduction.","relevant_to":"prop:exact-solution-reduction"},{"author":"Christoph Breunig and Jan Johannes","year":2011,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR","one_line":"Adaptive exact-solution NPIR comparator that remains the closest benchmark for the ordered-modulus conjecture.","relevant_to":"conj:adaptive-ordered-modulus"},{"author":"Xiaohong Chen and Markus Reiss","year":2011,"venue":"Econometric Theory","bibkey":"ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics","one_line":"Provides the Gaussian singular-coordinate lower-bound architecture and severe-ill-posed rate algebra informing the modulus and profile-rate statements.","relevant_to":"thm:functional-modulus"},{"author":"Peter Hall and Joel L. Horowitz","year":2006,"venue":"Annals of Statistics","bibkey":"HallHorowitz2006NonparametricMethodsIV","one_line":"Canonical mildly ill-posed NPIV rate algebra used for the polynomial profile-rate regime.","relevant_to":"prop:profile-rates"},{"author":"T. Tony Cai and Mark G. Low","year":2005,"venue":"Annals of Statistics","bibkey":"CaiLow2005AdaptationConfidenceIntervals","one_line":"Convex-class modulus machinery underlying the intrinsic converse benchmark for fixed linear functionals.","relevant_to":"thm:functional-modulus"},{"author":"Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Whitney Newey, Vasilis Syrgkanis, and Masatoshi Uehara","year":2022,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2022StronglyIdentifiedWeaklyIdentified","one_line":"Published dual/Riesz root-n criterion that the revised proposal now treats as a recovered equivalence rather than a strict tightening.","relevant_to":"prop:bennett-riesz-recovery"},{"author":"Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Whitney Newey, Vasilis Syrgkanis, and Masatoshi Uehara","year":2023,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023SourceConditionDR","one_line":"Qualification-sensitive inverse-problem functional inference comparator supporting the motivation for the adaptive and filter-comparison layer.","relevant_to":"conj:adaptive-ordered-modulus"}]}

banked_reason: No-solution NPIV functional minimax MODULUS is subsumed by exact-solution NPIR/linear-functional theory: the least-squares projection h-dagger exactly solves the projected normal equation T h = g^R, so the functional modulus, envelope, profile rates, Riesz boundary, and Lepski adaptation reduce to classical exact-solution functional-modulus results (Breunig-Johannes/Chen-Reiss/Cai-Low). D-0.5 v4 REJECT, all theorems already-known (N-pub). Genuine no-solution novelty (Shen et al.) lives in debiased-inference orthogonality, not the first-order modulus.
